description: Fixed-schema uncompressed image writer/parser modelled on a BMP/PPM-style image core: build/parseWidth/parseHeight/parse/validMagic over a frozen 7-byte header (magic "RGB" = [82,71,66], then a 16-bit big-endian width, then a 16-bit big-endian height) followed by a raw pixel payload. The two directions are pinned INDEPENDENTLY so they cannot collude on endianness: build is anchored by an exact-byte-layout law (its output equals the explicit frozen concatenation [82,71,66, w/256, w%256, h/256, h%256] ++ payload — frozen-op-anchored on ++ and the fixed-width byte arithmetic Nat.toUInt8 of w/256, w%256, h/256, h%256, a unique answer), while parse/parseWidth/parseHeight are anchored by an independent big-endian decode hi*256 + lo of their own header bytes on an ARBITRARY byte list, never through build. The headline obligations are the exact-layout law (build driver) and the independent field-extraction laws (parse drivers); magic rejection and header-length floors pin the failure paths. The parse-build round-trip is present but SUPPORTING ONLY — it is correctly demoted below the exact-layout and independent-decode drivers (here depth and guidance coincide). Scope: the schema is uncompressed and unpadded (the payload is appended verbatim with no row alignment, no color table, no file-size field); width and height are 16-bit big-endian; values are nonnegative Nat and bytes are UInt8; there is no Float and no variable-length integer anywhere. validMagic is a derived observer (a three-byte prefix check against the frozen magic tag), and a built image of an in-range (< 65536) dimension round-trips to the original dimension exactly.

apis.name: build
apis.type: Nat → Nat → List UInt8 → List UInt8

apis.name: parse
apis.type: List UInt8 → Option (Nat × Nat × List UInt8)
